methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Icarus is a seed-stage company in the Defense & Security sector. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparable companies: Stark Defence ($500M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), and Tenna Systems ($60M seed). This reflects a subset of a broader comp universe spanning 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, and Flock Safety.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach given available data. The median valuation across the four comparables is $225M, with a range spanning $60M to $500M. This single-method result implies an Icarus valuation of $225M. The triangulated range, incorporating sensitivity around the peer median, supports a low estimate of $191M, a median of $225M, and a high estimate of $259M.

The principal uncertainty stems from reliance on a single valuation method and a limited peer set of four companies. Stage and business-model alignment within the comparables is mixed—three peers are at Series A while only one (Tenna Systems) is at seed stage. Without revenue multiples, growth rates, or deal-specific terms, the analysis cannot isolate which peer characteristics (market position, burn rate, customer concentration) are most predictive of fair value for Icarus. The wide dispersion within the peer range ($60M–$500M) further suggests material sensitivity to peer selection and weighting methodology.
